name: SK Hynix
summary: SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer. **MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — operative price is Friday May 15 close (SK Hynix -7.7%). Helium-clock-expiry window NOW DAY 2 OF 14.** **Samsung 18-day strike May 21 — 4 days from start, no new resolution development over the weekend.** Korean fab complex three-simultaneous-supply-side-stressors continue: helium scarcity, Samsung strike from May 21, capacity-zero. Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside. **PRIOR (May 16):** SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer and the dominant HBM (high-bandwidth memory) supplier for AI accelerators. Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight. **JPMorgan estimates Samsung strike losses up to ₩43T ($28.8B); SK Hynix sits adjacent to the strike-window May 21-June 7 with capacity-zero constraint** — memory order-shift to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence; could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation but capacity-zero constraint limits upside. **Helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16; ~8 days remain at full production rates per March 24 framework). **SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike + helium clock-expiry stack is now operationally synchronised — three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** PRIOR (May 15): **A9 SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting (CONFIRMED — Tom's Hardware, TradingKey, Wccftech, ad-hoc-news, Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg). Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (each running hundreds of millions of dollars). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix is cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at potentially discounted prices could crimp future profitability. **Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030.** SK Hynix +144% YTD into May 14. SK Hynix has publicly claimed 'diversified supplies' on helium (CLAIMED — one-sided, NOT independently corroborated; TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED). **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23 and Samsung 18-day strike from May 21, SK Hynix sits at the center of the Korean fab complex's three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** **NEW TRIGGER**: 'SK Hynix announces supply guidance cut within 60 days' — would land helium-tsunami thesis. **Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron** flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could BENEFIT SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside.

current.price: SK Hynix peak +144% YTD into May 14; intraday -5%+ on May 15 as KOSPI dropped 6.1%
current.hbm_position: Dominant HBM supplier; Nvidia took lion's share of 2026 output
current.kospi_weight: Samsung + SK Hynix combined = 42.2% of KOSPI

active_triggers.condition: SK Hynix announces supply guidance cut or formal production halt within 60 days
SK Hynix accepts hyperscaler financing (Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google) within 90 days — would confirm capacity-zero structural reality

Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines. Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output. SK Hynix cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at discounted prices could crimp future profitability. **Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030.** SK Hynix +144% YTD into May 14. KOSPI -6.1% May 15 (intraday roundtrip from 8,046 to 7,493).
recent_signals.verification: Capacity 'essentially zero' = CONFIRMED (multi-source insider reporting Tom's Hardware + Wccftech + TradingKey + ad-hoc-news + Seoul Economic Daily + Bloomberg); hyperscaler financing proposals = CONFIRMED ACTION (Tom's Hardware primary); memory shortage through 2027-2030 = REPORTED (analyst estimate); SK Hynix 'diversified supplies' on helium = CLAIMED (one-sided).
